# Codex Instructions

<!-- BEGIN BEADS CODEX SETUP: generated by bd setup codex -->
## Beads Issue Tracker

Use Beads (`bd`) for durable task tracking in repositories that include it. Use the `beads` skill at `.agents/skills/beads/SKILL.md` (project install) or `~/.agents/skills/beads/SKILL.md` (global install) for Beads workflow guidance, then use the `bd` CLI for issue operations.

### Quick Reference

```bash
bd ready                # Find available work
bd show <id>            # View issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work
bd close <id>           # Complete work
bd prime                # Refresh Beads context
```

### Rules

- Use `bd` for all task tracking; do not create markdown TODO lists.
- Run `bd prime` when Beads context is missing or stale.
- Keep persistent project memory in Beads via `bd remember`; do not create ad hoc memory files.
<!-- END BEADS CODEX SETUP -->


Use this as the final `AGENTS.md` content:

## Markdown Math Formatting

Use Markdown-compatible math delimiters consistently.

- Inline math uses `$...$`.
- Display math uses `$$` on separate lines.
- Put one equation per display block where possible.
- Prefer renderer-safe single-line equations over complex alignment blocks.
- Do not use `\(...\)`, `\[...\]`, `\begin{equation}`, `\begin{align}`, `\label`, or `\tag`.
- Avoid custom/nonstandard macros such as `\ket`, `\bra`, `\braket`, and `\text`.
- Use explicit standard forms instead:
  - `|\psi\rangle` instead of `\ket{\psi}`
  - `\langle\psi|` instead of `\bra{\psi}`
  - `\langle\psi|\phi\rangle` instead of `\braket{\psi|\phi}`
  - `\mathrm{word}` or prose outside math instead of `\text{word}`
- Add blank lines before and after display math blocks.
- Escape literal dollar signs in prose as `\$`.

Good:

$$
dN = f_0 d\tau
$$

$$
\theta = S / \hbar
$$

Bad:

\[
dN = f_0 d\tau
\]

\begin{align}
\theta &= S / \hbar
\end{align}
